title: RFE - Report that configuration files are missing
body:
**Description**

I'm just making an issue so it doesn't get lost in the shuffle.  In #810 @rhatdan noted that we should at least throw a warning if the required [configuration files](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md#configuration-files) such as registry.conf, storage.conf, policy.json, mount.conf, etc are not found when running a Buildah command.

This is probably a good one for someone getting their feet wet in Buildah to take on.

comments.body: I think that's something valuable for Skopeo and Podman as well.

I agree. lets setup a library in buildah that checks this.
pkg/utils/
or something like that which validates all of the tools required for setup exists.

Why do we need this if the errors look pretty good and the tools actually work in some cases without the files.

Lets examine the errors.
# No registries.conf
```
 buildah from fedora:28
(image name "fedora:28" is a short name and no search registries are defined in /etc/containers/registries.conf): while pulling "fedora:28" as "localhost/fedora:28": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://localhost/fedora:28: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://localhost/v2/: dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
```

# No Policy.json
```
buildah from fedora:f28
The following failures happened while trying to pull image specified by "fedora:f28" based on search registries in /etc/containers/registries.conf:
* "localhost/fedora:f28": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
* "docker.io/library/fedora:f28": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
* "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:f28": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
* "quay.io/fedora:f28": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
* "registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:f28": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
* "registry.centos.org/fedora:f28": error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory
```

# No /etc/containers/storage.conf

```
# buildah from fedora:28
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 012cb44e3253: 85.49 MiB / 85.49 MiB [=========================] 26s
Copying config 4c5b21baa7a0: 1.99 KiB / 1.99 KiB [==========================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
fedora-working-container-1
```
I agree. I don't think this is necessary

I didn't double check before throwing up this RFE, just saw @rhatdan's request there to verify the file existence and didn't want to lose that thought before cleaning that issue up.  From what I see above, the verbiage could be better for registries.conf and policy.json.  I still think there's some value to at least printing out a warning if either of those files is not found with a message to that effect.
I think we have enough checks in the code now.
